entries.issue: Duplicate of narayanan2021efficient and narayanan2021megatron (identical paper, DOI 10.1145/3458817.3476209, SC21). Three different keys cite the same Megatron-LM paper, splitting citations silently. Also: key uses underscores (non-standard style); has both journal+booktitle fields (BibTeX confusion). Recommend consolidating to a single canonical key.

suggested_fix.qmd_propagation: Update all .qmd citations of narayanan_pipeline_parallelism_2021 and narayanan2021efficient to narayanan2021megatron

entries.issue: Duplicate of narayanan_pipeline_parallelism_2021 and narayanan2021megatron (same Megatron-LM SC21 paper, same DOI). @article entry type is incorrect for an SC21 conference paper; should be @inproceedings. Has both journal and booktitle fields.

suggested_fix.qmd_propagation: Update all .qmd citations of narayanan2021efficient to narayanan2021megatron

entries.issue: Cleanest of the three Megatron-LM duplicates; recommend keeping this as canonical and deleting the other two.
Duplicate of naumov2019dlrm (identical DLRM paper, arXiv:1906.00091, identical author list and metadata). Recommend keeping naumov2019dlrm (more descriptive key) and deleting this entry.

suggested_fix.qmd_propagation: Update all .qmd citations of naumov2019deep to naumov2019dlrm

entries.issue: Recommend keeping as canonical over naumov2019deep duplicate.
Title contains literal HTML/XML markup: '<scp>AI</scp> Literacy: ...'. The <scp> tag (small caps) is HTML, not LaTeX. This will render as literal '<scp>AI</scp>' in BibTeX output.

suggested_fix.from: <scp>AI</scp> Literacy: Definition, Teaching, Evaluation and Ethical Issues
suggested_fix.to: {AI} Literacy: {Definition}, Teaching, Evaluation and Ethical Issues

entries.issue: Mixed-case key (uppercase H100 suffix) is unusual but matches the artifact name (project-name pattern, acceptable per §5).
Confirmed: Pragmatic Bookshelf, March 2007, ISBN 0-9787392-1-3.
Has both journal (CoRR) and booktitle (Workshop on ML Systems at NIPS 2017) fields. Schema is mixed but renders correctly with @article type; minor hygiene issue.
Booktitle contains HTML entity '\&P' instead of proper LaTeX '\&P'. Will render as literal '&' in output.

suggested_fix.from: 2016 IEEE European Symposium on Security and Privacy (EuroS\&P)
suggested_fix.to: 2016 IEEE European Symposium on Security and Privacy (EuroS\&P)

entries.issue: Booktitle field is wrong: states 'National Institute of Standards and Technology, NIST Special Publication 800-220' but the paper is an arXiv preprint (2305.14384) submitted to the DataPerf challenge / Kaggle, not a NIST publication. The @article entry type with arxiv URL is fine; the spurious booktitle should be removed.

suggested_fix.reason: Wrong venue; this is an arXiv preprint, not a NIST publication

entries.issue: Duplicate of patarasuk2009bandwidth (identical paper, DOI 10.1016/j.jpdc.2008.09.002, JPDC bandwidth-optimal all-reduce). Two keys cite the same paper.

suggested_fix.qmd_propagation: Update all .qmd citations of patarasuk2009allreduce to patarasuk2009bandwidth

entries.issue: Recommend keeping as canonical over patarasuk2009allreduce duplicate. Has both journal and booktitle (same value) — minor hygiene issue.

Could not verify in 2 web searches. Title 'Carbon-Aware Computing for Sustainable AI' attributed to CACM vol 65 no 11 pp 50-58 (Nov 2022). The known Patterson sustainability paper from that era is 'Carbon Footprint of Machine Learning Training Will Plateau, Then Shrink' in IEEE Computer (already in patterson2022carbon). No matching CACM 2022 paper appears in Google Scholar / DBLP / ACM DL. Missing DOI and URL fields. Mixed-case key (capital P). Possibly a confused / fabricated entry.

suggested_fix.note: If real, add DOI and lowercase the key. If not real, delete and update .qmd citations.

entries.issue: Year mismatch between key and entry: key says 2007, entry has year=2006. The MIT Press book was published November 2006. Per §5 this is a 1-year gap (silent), but the key should match. Recommend renaming key to pfeifer2006body for consistency.

suggested_fix.qmd_propagation: Update all .qmd citations of pfeifer2007body to pfeifer2006body

entries.issue: Confirmed: N. LeRoy Poff, M.M. Brinson, J.W. Day (2002), Pew Center on Global Climate Change report. Title uses '\&' which is correct LaTeX escaping.

Year mismatch: key=2022 (arXiv preprint date 2022-01) but conference (ISPASS) date=2023-04. Preprint-vs-conference gap, silent per §5. Has both journal+booktitle fields, but @inproceedings type is correct.

Could not verify book exists. Searches for 'E-Waste and the Global Environment: The Hidden Cost of Discarded Electronics' by Jim Puckett at MIT Press 2016 return no matches. Puckett is a real e-waste activist (Basel Action Network, 'Exporting Harm' 2001 film) but the specific MIT Press book is not in catalogs. Missing ISBN, DOI, URL. Possibly misattributed.

suggested_fix.note: If real, add ISBN/URL. If not real, delete and find a real e-waste citation to replace.

entries.issue: Already stamped x-verified-by={claude-bib-audit-2026-05}; not re-audited per instructions.
Already stamped x-verified-by={claude-bib-audit-2026-05}; not re-audited per instructions.
Has both journal and booktitle. @inproceedings type is correct for SC20.

PAPER HAS BEEN RETRACTED BY IEEE. The IEEE Xplore page (DOI 10.1109/iceca.2018.8474730) explicitly shows 'Retracted: Secure boot of Embedded Applications - A Review'. Citing a retracted paper as an authoritative source is a serious editorial issue. Author format is also unusual ('R.V., Rashmi' suggests Rashmi is given name, R.V. surname-initials).

suggested_fix.note: Find a non-retracted secure-boot review to cite instead. Update .qmd files using rashmi2018secure to use the replacement key. Consider rashmi2018secure-retracted note if keeping as a counter-example.

entries.issue: Booktitle contains HTML entity '\&' instead of proper LaTeX '\&'. Renders as literal '&' in output.

suggested_fix.from: Proceedings of the 26th ACM SIGKDD International Conference on Knowledge Discovery \& Data Mining
suggested_fix.to: Proceedings of the 26th ACM SIGKDD International Conference on Knowledge Discovery \& Data Mining

entries.issue: Title is just 'Snorkel' with subtitle 'rapid training data creation with weak supervision' as separate field. Acceptable convention; combined output renders correctly.

Title typography is malformed: '"" Why should i trust you?"" Explaining the predictions of any classifier'. Doubled ASCII single quotes (LaTeX-style left/right quotes attempted but used wrong direction; should be ``...''). Also lowercase 'i' instead of 'I'. The actual paper title is '"Why Should I Trust You?": Explaining the Predictions of Any Classifier'.

suggested_fix.from: '' Why should i trust you?'' Explaining the predictions of any classifier
suggested_fix.to: ``Why Should {I} Trust You?'': Explaining the Predictions of Any Classifier

entries.issue: Project-name key (RT-2 model name); first author is Brohan, but key uses artifact name. Acceptable per §5 corporate/project-name pattern (similar to alexnet2012).

Could not verify in 2 web searches. Title 'Tech Industry Trends in Hardware Lock-In and Their Sustainability Implications' attributed to 'Sustainable Computing Journal' vol 10 no 1 pp 34-50 (2022). No journal by that exact name appears in any indexing service. Missing DOI and URL. Single-author 'Russell, Mark' with no further identifying info. Possibly fabricated.

suggested_fix.note: If real, add DOI/URL/publisher. If not real, delete and find a real hardware-lock-in sustainability citation.

entries.issue: Has both journal (arXiv preprint) and booktitle (NeurIPS 2019 EMC^2 workshop). Both venues are real for this paper.
Already stamped x-verified-by={claude-bib-audit-2026-05}; not re-audited per instructions.
Has both journal and booktitle (same SIGMETRICS venue). @article type is correct.
Confirmed: Philip Schwan, Lustre paper at 2003 Linux Symposium (kernel.org/doc/ols/2003).

top_3_broken.issue: Cited paper has been formally RETRACTED by IEEE. Editorial integrity issue: textbook should not present a retracted paper as an authoritative reference without explicit framing.

Three different keys cite the same Megatron-LM SC21 paper (DOI 10.1145/3458817.3476209). Citations split silently across keys; readers cannot tell they reference the same work. Same triplication issue affects naumov2019deep/dlrm and patarasuk2009allreduce/bandwidth (5 more duplicate keys total).

Three entries that cannot be verified against any standard index (Google Scholar, DBLP, ACM DL, MIT Press catalog). All lack DOI/URL fields. Pattern suggests fabrication or severe misattribution. If used in prose, citations point to non-existent works.
